title: thalictrum simplex subsp. bauhinii
intro: Thalictrum simplex subsp. bauhinii, also known as Meadow Rue, is a perennial plant species in the family Ranunculaceae, characterized by its delicate, fern-like foliage and tall spikes of small, nodding white flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Meadow Rue include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, making it a popular herbal remedy for skin conditions, infections, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Meadow Rue include teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used externally to soothe skin irritations and internally to support immune function.
